post.description:
Start a device authorization. Returns a device_code for polling and a short
user_code to approve in the mobile app. Codes expire after a few minutes.

post.description: Poll a device authorization. Once approved, returns an API token exactly once.

get.description:
Look up a pending device authorization by its user code, so the app can show
what is being authorized before the user confirms.

post.summary: Approve a device authorization. The polling device then receives an API token
post.description:
Approve a device authorization. The polling device then receives an API token
scoped to the approving user.

post.summary: Mint a short-lived access JWT for a target user, using a master credential.
post.description:
Mint a short-lived access JWT for a target user, using a master credential.

Used for impersonation (admin, ops, debug, scripted tools). Authentication
is via HTTP Basic with token_id as user and secret as password. The minted
JWT has a 10-minute TTL and no refresh token is issued. Every successful
mint writes a row to `others.master_token_audit`.

patch.description:
Updates the session data
If a field is set to None, it is ignored

A user can edit a training session if:
 - he created it
 - he is in the same group as the training session

schema.title: Response Load The Samples For The Given Training Session V1 Training Session Samples Get

get.summary: Load the lap structure for the given training session.
get.description:
Load the lap structure for the given training session.

granularity="laps" (default) returns the device's interval/rep laps. granularity="length"
returns per-pool-length swim splits reconstructed from the 1 s distance channel (~1 s precision,
finer than the samples endpoint); it is empty for anything but a pool swim with a known length.

schema.title: Response Load The Lap Structure For The Given Training Session  V1 Training Session Laps Get

get.description: Get the sessions of a user

schema.title: Response Get The Sessions Of A User V1 Training Sessions Get

post.description:
Merges 2 training sessions together.
The fields from `session_to` will be overwritten with the `session_from` if
they are not null in `session_from`.
If `session_to` is linked to as 3rd party session, it will be removed and replaced
by `session_from`

Eg:

session_from:
    id 1
    ts_start 10:00:00
    ts_end   11:00:00
    calories  20
    bpm null
    tags [1, 2]
    exercises: [exo1, exo2...]

session_to:
    id 2
    ts_start 09:00:00
    ts_end   10:00:00
    calories  10
    bpm 10
    tags [2, 3]
    exercises: [exo3, exo4...]

the output will be:
    id 2
    ts_start 10:00:00
    ts_end   11:00:00
    calories  20
    bpm 10
    tags [2, 3]
    exercises: [exo3, exo4...]

schema.title: Response Get An Exercise V1 Training Exercise Get

patch.description:
Updates the specified fields
Note:
   -  only the url media can be updated here. To update
    other medias like video or images, call the
    /media/exercise/upload (to add ) or /media/exercise (to delete)
    endpoints
    - only the specified field will be updated.
        eg: if only "name" is in the body, only "name" will change

get.description:
Per-sport activity-detail metric overrides.

Returns the default ordered list of metric keys plus per-sport overrides
keyed by sport_id. The mobile client looks up its session's `sport_id`
in `overrides` and falls back to `default` when absent.
